headlineSummary: Anthropic and OpenAI skew negative with a close tie between them, while Google Gemini is mostly neutral to mildly positive on limited evidence [E4].

ranking.summary: Anthropic drew broadly negative sentiment, with strong criticism around business strategy, trust, safety, and model quality, though a few comments were positive and it remains in a close tie with OpenAI [E1][E2][E6][E7][E8][E9][E10].
OpenAI was also viewed negatively, mainly for execution and product quality concerns, with one mild positive comparison but still a close tie with Anthropic [E4][E5][E9][E10].
Google Gemini saw a small amount of positive sentiment, described as a good model and the stable choice, but the signal is limited evidence [E4].

evidence.excerpt: Anthropic made a mistake in cutting off third-party clients

annotations.rationale: The title explicitly says Anthropic 'made a mistake,' a clear negative judgment about its strategy.

evidence.excerpt: While Anthropic was within their right to enforce their ToS, the move has changed my perspective. In the language of moats and lock-ins, it all makes sense, sure, but as a potential sign of the shape of things to come, it has hurt my trust in CC as something I want to build on top of.

annotations.rationale: The commenter explicitly says Anthropic's move 'has hurt my trust,' which is clear negative trust sentiment.

evidence.excerpt: I feel like Anthropic is probably making the right choice here.

annotations.rationale: The commenter explicitly endorses Anthropic's decision as 'the right choice,' a mild positive view of its strategy.

evidence.excerpt: If nothing else, this was likely driven by Google being the most stable of the AI labs. Gemini is objectively a good model (whether it's #1 or #5 in ranking aside) so Apple can confidently deliver a good (enough) product. Also for Apple, they know their provider has ridiculously deep pockets, a good understanding and infrastructure in place for large enterprises, and a fairly diversified revenue stream. Going with Anthropic or OpenAI, despite on the surface having that clean Apple smell and feel, carries a lot of risk Apple's part. Both companies are far underwater, liable to take risks, and liable to drown if they even fall a bit behind.

annotations.rationale: The excerpt explicitly calls Gemini 'objectively a good model' and frames Google as the stable choice.
Anthropic is described as a risky choice, 'far underwater,' and liable to 'drown,' which is strong negative business sentiment.
OpenAI is grouped with Anthropic as a risky provider that is 'far underwater' and may 'drown,' indicating strong negative business sentiment.

evidence.excerpt: OpenAI had it, they had the foot in the door with their integration last year with Siri. But they dropped that ball and many other balls.

annotations.rationale: The commenter says OpenAI 'dropped that ball and many other balls,' a direct criticism of execution.

evidence.excerpt: I had Claude Code dig into it using an 11 MB HAR of a session that reproduces the problem, and it used grep and sed(!) to find the issue in just under 5 minutes (4m56s).

annotations.rationale: The commenter reports Claude Code solved a real debugging task quickly and effectively, indicating strong praise.

evidence.excerpt: Sadly they haven't completely solved that yet. Instead their help page at https://support.claude.com/en/articles/13364135-using-cowork... tells users "Avoid granting access to local files with sensitive information, like financial documents" and "Monitor Claude for suspicious actions that may indicate prompt injection".

annotations.rationale: The commenter says Claude's safety issue is not solved and criticizes Anthropic's guidance around prompt-injection risk.

evidence.excerpt: What do the words "if it's instructed to" mean here? It seems like Claude can in fact delete files whenever it wants regardless of instruction.

annotations.rationale: The comment alleges Claude may delete files regardless of instruction, a severe safety criticism.

evidence.excerpt: I find it surprising that GPT managed to complete (with indeed a harness that doesn't seem very powerful!) and Claude still cannot.

annotations.rationale: GPT is credited with completing the task in contrast to Claude, which is mild positive comparative sentiment for OpenAI.
Claude is criticized for still being unable to complete the task in a direct comparison.

evidence.excerpt: I've tried ChatGpt and Claude on datasheets of electronic components, and I'm sorry to say that they are awful at it.

annotations.rationale: The commenter says ChatGPT is 'awful at it' for datasheet reading, a strong negative quality judgment.
The commenter says Claude is 'awful at it' for datasheet reading, a strong negative quality judgment.
